title: [Non-Record] Modified LLM-JEPA pretraining from scratch — 1.2699 bpbAdd int6 quantization lzma
author: IshiPareek
body:
## Summary

A modified implementation of JEPA (Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture) for language model pretraining from scratch in the Parameter Golf challenge.
val_bpb: 1.2699 | 8×H100 SXM | 600s | Seed 1337

## Architecture

Two parallel encoders run on the full sequence every step:
- Context encoder — standard 9-layer transformer, trained via gradients
- Target encoder — EMA copy of context encoder, no gradients
  target = 0.996 × target + 0.004 × context (updated every step)
- JEPAPredictor — small 2-layer MLP mapping context → predicted embedding

Loss = CE loss + 0.1 × MSE(normalize(predicted_emb), normalize(target_emb))

## Results

| Step | val_bpb |
|------|---------|
| 1000 | 1.3831  |
| 2000 | 1.3248  |
| 3000 | 1.3010  |
| 4000 | 1.2855  |
| 5000 | 1.2776  |
| 6000 | 1.2702  |
| 7000 | 1.2646  |
| final (post-quantization) | 1.2699 |

## Size
Model serializes to ~73MB (target encoder removed before serialization — 
not needed for inference). Over 16MB record track limit — submitted as 
non-record.

Compression work done:
- int6 quantization (replacing int8) — saves ~25%
- LZMA compression (replacing zlib) — marginal additional saving
- Estimated compressed size: ~52MB

## Key Findings
- JEPA learns consistently — loss curve healthy throughout
- Architecture is stable — no collapse, no crashes  
- Full JEPA at 16MB is fundamentally constrained by dual encoder size
- Next step: mid-layer JEPA (target encoder layers 0-5 only)

## Why This Matters
Standard LM training penalizes valid synonyms equally — "down" is as 
wrong as "purple" after "the cat sat". JEPA predicts in embedding space 
where meaning clusters — "on", "down", "there" are all correct continuations 
and share similar embeddings.

## References
- LLM-JEPA: https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.14252
- I-JEPA: https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.08243

Submission: records/track_non_record_16mb/2026-04-08_JEPA_baseline/
submission_readme:
# Non-record: LeakyReLU(0.5)^2 + TrigramHash on PR 414 stack

val_bpb: 1.3762 | 1xA100, 7000 iterations

## Built on
PR 414 by signalrush (11L EMA + GPTQ-lite + warmdown3500 + QAT@0.15)

## Changes

### 1. LeakyReLU(0.5)^2
Replaced relu^2 with LeakyReLU(0.5)^2 in MLP.
Motivation: keeps neurons alive during training instead of permanently killing them.

### 2. TrigramHash
Groups 3 consecutive tokens into 8192 buckets before attention.
Gives each layer richer local context.

## Results
Hardware: 1xA100
Iterations: 7000
val_bpb: 1.3762

Note: not comparable to 8xH100 leaderboard submissions.

## Next steps
- Depth-based precision per layer
- Novel layer interaction mechanisms
- Full 8xH100 run once validated
